title: Buyer
description: **Buyer – Retail, Front of Shop \- Hybrid in Dublin 24 (3 days in office)** This is not a “sit behind a desk and analyse spreadsheets” buying role. We’re partnering with a **large, fast‑moving Irish retail group (300\+ stores)** that is reshaping how it buys, ranges and launches branded products across its estate. They’re now hiring a **commercial Buyer** to take **full ownership of a Front of Shop category,** from supplier negotiation right through to in‑store activation. * **You own your category** * **You negotiate directly with global brands** * **You control promotions, space, launches and margin** * **You are accountable for the commercial number** If you’re looking **to step up in your career as a Buyer** , or are **frustrated by too many layers and not enough autonomy** , this is a genuine career‑accelerating opportunity. **What will I be doing?** * Owning the **end‑to‑end commercial cycle** for assigned categories * Negotiating with established global suppliers * Driving **promotional cycles** , JBPs, margin and sell‑through * Securing **exclusive launches, GWP activity and first‑to‑market deals** * Working closely with wholesale and retail teams to leverage buying power * Managing planograms, space allocation and range performance * Representing your category at weekly trade and commercial meetings **What experience do I need to be eligible?** * **2\+ years’ experience** in buying or assistant buying * Experience working with **branded supplier‑led categories** (e.g. Beauty, FMCG, Confectionery, Health, Electrical, Power Tools, etc.) * Comfortable **owning a P\&L number** * Can work independently in a **lean, hands‑on environment** **This opportunity is a full\-time permanent role, based in Dublin 24, hybrid (3 days onsite).** **To hear more about this position or to discuss your suitability, apply today and we will be in touch with more information.** **By applying here you agree to RECRUITERS storing your CV in our secure applicant tracking system (ATS; Bullhorn). As your consultants, we are dedicated to helping you find a new job in your field of expertise and, equally, to keeping your personal information secure at all times. Your CV will not be sent to any third party without your consent.**

title: Cash Management Analyst
description: **Senior Analyst – Currency Management** Dublin \| Hybrid Working ***\*Currency Management experience is essential for this opportunity\**** A leading global investment management firm is looking to hire a Senior Analyst to join its Currency Management team in Dublin. This is a strong opportunity for someone with investment operations or middle office experience looking to move into a highly visible FX and exposure management role within a complex international environment. **Responsibilities** * Monitor fund cash balances, securities holdings and FX exposures across a range of investment products including Fixed Income, Equity, Multi\-Asset and Fund\-of\-Funds structures. * Generate FX orders including spot, forward and NDF transactions to manage cash and hedge currency exposure. * Work closely with Portfolio Managers, Investment teams, Trading, Risk and Compliance teams on FX exposure management and funding requirements. * Investigate and analyse hedged product performance and explain deviations from expected outcomes. * Support fund launches, closures, strategic initiatives and process improvement projects. * Produce and analyse management information, identifying trends, risks and anomalies for senior stakeholders. * Contribute to operational efficiency initiatives with a focus on risk reduction and process enhancement. **Requirements** * Strong experience within Investment Operations, Treasury, Middle Office, Currency Management, or a related environment. * Strong understanding of financial markets and foreign exchange products. * Experience managing cash balances, FX exposure, liquidity, or reconciliations within an investment management or banking environment. * Excellent analytical and numerical skills with strong attention to detail. * Ability to communicate effectively with senior stakeholders and work in a fast\-paced environment. * Experience with Charles River or similar OMS platforms is advantageous. **What’s on Offer** * Competitive compensation package * Strong internal progression opportunities * Hybrid working model * Exposure to global investment teams and complex multi\-asset products * Collaborative and high\-performing environment

title: Chef De Partie
description: **Location** Jeremiah Grant **About the Role** We are looking for a strong and reliable Chef de Partie to join our busy pub and restaurant kitchen. This is a fast\-paced environment where consistency, speed, and teamwork are essential. A high level of training will be provided but a desire to progress in a career in the culinary arts is essential. You will be responsible for running your own section and delivering high\-quality dishes during busy service periods. **Key Responsibilities** * Run a designated section of the kitchen * Prepare and cook dishes to a high standard * Work efficiently during busy services * Maintain cleanliness and organisation in your area * Support junior team members **What We’re Looking For** * Ability to work in a high\-volume environment * Strong work ethic and reliability * Positive attitude and team player **Why Join Us?** * Busy, energetic kitchen with strong team culture * Opportunity to develop and progress * Supportive management team **Summary** If you are a hardworking and thrives in a fast\-paced environment and takes pride in delivering quality food, we want to hear from you. **How to Apply** Please send your CV and a brief note outlining your experience. Work Location: In person

title: Chief Operating Officer (PCF-42)
description: Who we are: TrueLayer is Europe’s fastest\-growing Pay by Bank network. We power smarter, safer and faster payments by combining real\-time bank payments with financial and identity data. E\-commerce, iGaming and Financial Services businesses use our products to onboard new users, accept money and make payouts in seconds, and at scale. Live across 22 countries and with 1 new user joining TrueLayer’s Pay by Bank network every 3 seconds, we’re in a very exciting growth stage. This is why we’re expanding the team across our offices in London, Milan, and Dublin. Listed in Sifted as one of the 100 fastest growing start\-ups in UK \& Ireland, and CNBC’s World Top Fintech Companies, we’re trusted by industry leaders like RyanAir and Just Eat Takeaway for continued innovation within our payment products. To date, we’ve raised over $300 million from world\-renowned investors including Stripe, Tiger Global, Northzone, and Tencent. But this is just the beginning. We’re looking for people who are excited about joining the Pay by Bank revolution and helping us change the way the world pays. Could this be you? **Description** **The Role: Chief Operations Officer (PCF\-42\)** We are seeking an EU Chief Operating Officer (PCF\-42\) to join TrueLayer Ireland Limited (TIL) \- TrueLayer’s EU regulated entity. Reporting directly to the CEO, this role is responsible for the overall operational management and control of the Irish regulated entity. As a visible leader in our Dublin headquarters, you will champion TrueLayer’s high\-performance culture and foster local collaboration. The COO will form part of the management team for TIL’s EU operation, supporting the Truelayer Ireland CEO to lead the vision, strategy, and operations of TrueLayer Ireland with a special focus on operational resilience and outsourced functions ensuring they are effectively managed and fully aligned with relevant local regulatory requirements. As part of the wider TrueLayer business operations function, you will also support a number of cross\-functional group\-wide projects and business initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** **Outsourcing and Third\-Party Management** * Accountable for the design, implementation, and ultimate effectiveness of the firm’s Outsourcing Framework. This includes chairing the Outsourcing Oversight Committee and ensuring that all intra\-group arrangements provide sufficient operational continuity to meet Truelayer’s international expansion goals. * Ensure outsourcing and critical third\-party operational arrangements comply with CBI outsourcing and operational risk expectations by working closely with outsourced functions across Business Operations, Risk, Compliance, Financial Crime, Infosec, Legal, Product and Commercial to align operations with business needs. **Operational Resilience** * Responsible for ensuring the management body defines, approves, and oversees a comprehensive ICT risk management framework and digital operational resilience strategy. * Ensure compliance with CBI expectations on operational resilience, business continuity planning, scenario testing, crisis communications, wind down planning and disaster recovery. * Create and maintain mapping and visualisation of all operational resilience frameworks, critical business processes, interdependencies, and interconnections to support the resilient delivery of Important Business Services and Critical Business Functions under DORA requirements. * Ensure operational dependencies and vulnerabilities are identified, documented, and managed. * Understanding our processes from the ground up to suggest improvement areas. **Governance \& Board Engagement** * Report directly to the Truelayer Ireland CEO as the COO of the EU regulated entity. * Work directly with the CEO to provide operational assurance by providing accurate, timely, and meaningful operational reporting to the Board and senior management. * Translate strategic objectives into executable operational plans, performance metrics, resource allocation frameworks, and governance structures. * Ensure effective internal controls, segregation of duties, and oversight mechanisms are in place and operating effectively. **Data\-Driven Performance** * Report Executive\-level Management Information (MI) and performance metrics to the business and Board of Directors. Use data\-driven insights to identify business drivers, improve operational efficiency to improve performance, effectiveness, and efficiency. * Champion the use of AI tools to automate manual workflows, streamline high\-volume reporting cycles, and enhance management information (MI) accuracy across both Group and entity levels. * Ensure continuous monitoring of key performance metrics reporting internally on operational health KPIs. **About You** * Previous experience in a PCF\-42 or equivalent regulated senior operations role within a regulated financial services or fintech environment. * Demonstrate a ‘can\-do’ attitude with a willingness to be hands\-on in a scaling environment, ensuring operational tasks are executed effectively alongside high\-level strategic management. * Strong understanding of CBI regulatory frameworks, Operational Resilience, DORA and Outsourcing and Third Party requirements. * Experience engaging with the Central Bank of Ireland or other relevant regulatory authorities, auditors, and senior stakeholders. * Strong vendor management skills with a proven ability to manage third party relationships and outsourced functions. * Ability to operate effectively within a management team and engage with Board\-level stakeholders. * Not afraid to get stuck in and build things from the ground up. * Proactively identifying improvement areas and creating processes to support future growth and expansion. * Seeking out opportunities to add value and improve speed of execution for the operations of the team. **We would be particularly excited if you have** * A proven track record of managing a third\-party and service delivery to ensure critical outsourcing arrangements strictly align with CBI and DORA regulatory mandates. * Experience managing Outsourcing, Operational Resilience and EU DORA frameworks. * Track record of implementing AI\-driven tools and optimising complex workflows to eliminate friction. * Experience scaling regulated operational infrastructure in a fast\-paced fintech environment. **Please note: This is a Pre\-Approval Controlled Function (PCF\-42\) under the Central Bank of Ireland’s (CBI) Fitness \& Probity regime and is subject to regulatory approval.** **Don’t meet all the requirements?** Multiple studies have shown that women and people of underrepresented groups are sometimes less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every single requirement. At TrueLayer, we are committed to building a diverse workforce, so if you are excited about this role and have the essential skills, but not the entire checklist \- we’d still love to hear from you! **Benefits** What you can expect from us * Meaningful equity in the company 💰 * Flexible hours and hybrid working. Split your time 50/50 between working from home and our welcoming offices in Dublin 🇮🇪 spending 2\-3 days per week in the office. * Need to collect the kids from childcare? Love a workout in the gym first thing? No worries, we trust you to do your best work within our hybrid framework 🧘 * 24 days holiday as standard ✈️ with flexible bank holidays, so you can take those days whenever you like 🌍 * 12 fully\-paid wellbeing days a year and your birthday off (on top of the holiday allowance) 🕊️ * 2 volunteering days to support causes important to you * Life Cover as part of our benefits package, offering financial protection and peace of mind for you and your loved ones 🛟 * 90 day ‘work from abroad’ policy 👩‍💻☀️ * Generous parental leave, above and beyond statutory requirements 👪 * Competitive pension contribution at 4% \& 4% 🧓 * Private health insurance from the day you start 🧑‍⚕️ * Membership of mental wellbeing platform Spill and premium Calm subscription * A €1000 budget to spend on learning \& development each year 📚 * Free lunch from Just Eat 🥙 up to 3 days a week when you are in the office At TrueLayer, we don’t just do inclusion and diversity. We embrace people that have different opinions, perspectives and personalities. Because we believe that by seeing the world from all sorts of angles, we can make life better for all the people who live in it. We strongly encourage applications from underrepresented groups (e.g. people of colour, the LGBTQ\+ community, people with disabilities, neurodivergent people, parents, carers, and people from all socio\-economic backgrounds). If you’d like to discuss alternative working patterns, please let us know. We will always aim to make appropriate adjustments to ensure we are fully inclusive to people with different needs during our interview process. So if you need us to make any adjustments to suit your individual needs please let us know \- we’ll be happy to support you.

title: Clerk of Works
description: At Jacobs, we're challenging today to reinvent tomorrow by solving the world's most critical problems for thriving cities, resilient environments, mission\-critical outcomes, operational advancement, scientific discovery and cutting\-edge manufacturing, turning abstract ideas into realities that transform the world for good. Your impact As a Clerk of Works professional, you will play a key role in supporting the Project, ensuring construction works are delivered safely, efficiently and in full compliance with contract requirements, specifications and regulatory standards. You will operate at the heart of site delivery, providing construction assurance, overseeing workmanship and quality, and acting as a trusted interface between site teams, project leadership and key stakeholders. You’ll also contribute to the establishment and leadership of the Engineering Assurance sub‑function, supporting high‑performance delivery on a technically complex scheme. You will bring a strong site‑based background with over five years’ experience in construction supervision or management, ideally on major road or infrastructure schemes. You’re comfortable operating at the sharp end of delivery—inspecting works, challenging standards where needed, and ensuring everything on site aligns with specifications, regulations and best practice. From earthworks and drainage to bridges and structures, you understand what good looks like and you know how to uphold it in a live, urban construction environment. This role is ideal for a hands‑on professional who enjoys being close to the work, values quality and safety, and is motivated by delivering infrastructure that will benefit communities for generations. * Assist the NEC4 Project Supervisor in overseeing all construction assurance activities on site * Inspect and monitor workmanship, quality and safety across civil works including earthworks, drainage, bridges and structures * Ensure works comply with Works Requirements, specifications, Irish Building Regulations and Health \& Safety standards * Support the establishment and leadership of the Engineering Assurance sub‑functional area * Contribute to financial, resource and programme planning for the scheme * Coordinate and oversee day‑to‑day activities of Clerks of Works within the team * Act as a key point of contact between site, project core teams and relevant stakeholders * Encourage knowledge transfer and collaboration across consultant and client teams * Support reporting requirements in line with governance and PMO expectations * Embed a culture of continuous improvement and best practice across site activities Here's what you'll need * 5\+ years’ experience in construction management / supervision on large‑scale road or infrastructure projects * Strong knowledge of civil and structural works and the ability to interpret drawings and specifications * Experience working within Irish Building Regulations, planning processes and Health \& Safety requirements * A proactive, solutions‑focused approach with excellent attention to detail * Strong communication skills and the ability to build effective working relationships * The confidence to manage multiple priorities and contribute to team leadership **Qualifications** * Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering (or equivalent) * Safepass accreditation * Chartered / Incorporated / Associate Engineer status or working towards same (desirable) *Why Join Jacobs \| What We Offer* At Jacobs, you’ll find more than a job — you’ll find a career with purpose. Meaningful Work Be part of a landmark infrastructure project that will shape the future of transport and communities across Ireland. Long‑Term Career Security This is a long‑duration project role, backed by Jacobs’ strong and growing infrastructure pipeline in Ireland and globally. People‑First Culture We foster an inclusive, supportive and collaborative workplace, where your ideas are valued and your contribution matters. Professional Development We invest in your future through * Defined career pathways * Access to global technical networks * Support for professional accreditation and continuous learning * Opportunities to work across diverse, high‑profile projects Global Reach, Local Impact You’ll benefit from the stability and resources of a global organisation, while working within a close‑knit, locally focused team in Dublin and across Ireland Reward \& Wellbeing Jacobs offers a competitive reward package and places strong emphasis on wellbeing, flexibility and sustainable performance. Joining Jacobs not only connects you locally but globally. Our values stand on a foundation of safety, integrity, inclusion and belonging. We put people at the heart of our business, and we truly believe that by supporting one another through our culture of caring, we all succeed. We value positive mental health and a sense of belonging for all employees. With safety and flexibility always top of mind, we’ve gone beyond traditional ways of working so you have the support, means and space to maximize your potential. You’ll uncover flexible working arrangements, benefits, and opportunities, from well\-being benefits to our global giving and volunteering program, to exploring new and inventive ways to help our clients make the world a better place. No matter what drives you, you’ll discover how you can cultivate, nurture, and achieve your goals – all at a single global company. Find out more about life at Jacobs. We aim to embed inclusion and belonging in everything we do. We know that if we are inclusive, we’re more connected and creative. We accept people for who they are, and we champion the richness of different perspectives, lived experiences and backgrounds in the workplace, as a source of learning and innovation. We are committed to building vibrant communities within Jacobs, including through our Jacobs Employee Networks, Communities of Practice and our Find Your Community initiatives, allowing every employee to find connection, purpose, and belonging. Find out more about our Jacobs Employee Networks here. Jacobs partners with VERCIDA to help us attract and retain talent from a wide range of backgrounds. For greater online accessibility please visit https//www.vercida.com/uk/employers/jacobs to view and access our roles. As a disability confident employer, we will interview disabled candidates who best meet the criteria. We welcome applications from candidates who are seeking flexible working and from those who may not meet all the listed requirements for a role. Onsite employees are expected to attend a Jacobs Workplace on a full\-time basis, as required by the nature of their role. Your application experience is important to us, and we’re keen to adapt to make every interaction even better. If you require further support or reasonable adjustments with regards to the recruitment process (for example, you require the application form in a different format), please contact the team via Careers Support.

title: Client Engineer - Star Trek Fleet Command
description: Scopely is looking for a Client Engineer to join our Star Trek Fleet Command team within our Live Games Portfolio division in Dublin. At Scopely, we care deeply about what we do and want to inspire play every day \- whether in our work environments alongside our talented colleagues or through our deep connections with our communities of players. We are a global team of game lovers who are developing, publishing and innovating the mobile games industry, connecting millions of people around the world daily. Star Trek Fleet Command is a standout success in the gaming industry. Launched in 2018, our in\-house developed 4X Strategy MMO has continually evolved, earning its place as one of the top\-grossing games on the market. ### **What You Will Do** As a Client Engineer focused on Core Tech and Platform Engineering, you’ll contribute to the ongoing evolution and stability of our Unity\-based client. This role focuses on the systems, tooling, and infrastructure that support the game experience across devices and platforms. You will work closely with senior engineers and cross\-functional teams to improve performance, maintainability, and scalability across the client technology stack. * Contribute to the development and maintenance of shared client systems such as asset loading, UI systems, tooling, memory tracking, patching, and performance monitoring. * Help identify and resolve performance, stability, and scalability issues across the client. * Collaborate with Backend, DevOps, QA, and gameplay teams to improve workflows, integrations, and development processes. * Follow and contribute to coding standards, architectural patterns, and engineering best practices. * Support Unity upgrades and platform integrations across Android, iOS, and PC. * Assist in improving the modularity, reliability, and maintainability of the codebase. * Participate in code reviews, technical discussions, and collaborative problem\-solving. ### **What We're Looking For** * 3\+ years of professional experience with Unity and C\# development. * Experience working on game systems, shared frameworks, tools, or platform\-related features. * Solid debugging and problem\-solving skills, including investigating performance and memory\-related issues. * Understanding of Unity systems such as AssetBundles, scene management, rendering, or lifecycle management. * Experience developing for mobile platforms (iOS/Android) and familiarity with profiling/debugging tools such as Unity Profiler, Android Profiler, or Xcode Instruments. * Familiarity with version control systems and CI/CD workflows such as Git, Jenkins, or GitHub Actions. * Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively across disciplines. * BSc degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or equivalent practical experience. ### **Bonus Points** * Experience supporting Unity version upgrades or large\-scale client migrations. * Exposure to performance optimisation and memory management techniques. * Familiarity with networking technologies such as WebSockets, Protobuf, or gRPC. * Experience with native plugins or platform\-specific integrations. * Knowledge of LiveOps systems, telemetry, or experimentation frameworks. * Understanding of backend\-client coordination and data synchronisation. * Exposure to mobile development challenges such as device fragmentation, app store requirements, or patching systems. At Scopely, we create games for everyone and we want to ensure that the people behind our games reflect that! We are committed to creating a diverse, supportive work environment where everyone is treated with respect. We are committed to providing equal employment opportunities and welcome individuals from all backgrounds to join us \& embrace the adventure! ### **About Scopely** Scopely is a leading video game and global interactive entertainment company, home to many of the world’s most beloved and enduring experiences, including two of the most successful mobile games of all\-time “MONOPOLY GO!” and “Pokémon GO,” along with “Stumble Guys,” “Star Trek™ Fleet Command,” “MARVEL Strike Force,” “WWE Champions,” the Scrabble® franchise, “Yahtzee® With Buddies,” and many others. Across mobile, web, PC, and console, Scopely creates, develops, publishes, and live\-operates one of the most diversified and award\-winning portfolios in the games industry — bringing hundreds of millions of players together through a shared love of play. Founded in 2011, Scopely is powered by its exceptional team — including thousands of world\-class gamemakers around the globe, a distinctive tenet\-driven culture, and its proprietary technology platform, Playgami. Together, these strengths have fueled Scopely’s position as the \#1 mobile games company in the U.S. and \#2 globally, generating more than $10 billion in lifetime revenue. Whether building global sensations like “MONOPOLY GO!” from the ground up, or expanding through strategic acquisitions, including the FoxNext, GSN, and Niantic games businesses — Scopely consistently delivers experiences players love today and return to for years to come. Recognized multiple times as one of the "100 Most Influential Companies in the World" by TIME magazine and one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies" and “Best Workplaces for Innovators,” Scopely believes that video games can be a force for good — creating meaningful connections, vibrant communities, and making life better through play. Scopely has global operations and partners across four continents in more than a dozen countries worldwide. For more information, visit: https://www.scopely.com/. Notice to Candidates: Scopely will never request payment or financial information during the application or hiring process. Please apply only through our official website and verify that all Talent Partner communications come from an email address ending in @scopely.com. Should you have any questions or encounter any fraudulent requests/emails/websites, please immediately contact recruiting@scopely.com. Our job applicant privacy policies are available here: California Privacy Notice and EEA/UK Privacy Notice. Employment at Scopely is based solely on a person's merit and qualifications. Scopely does not discriminate against any employee or applicant because of race, creed, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition (including breastfeeding), or any other basis protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law.

title: Commercial Graduate Rotation Program - Benelux Market
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* **About Futureforce University Recruiting** Our Futureforce University Recruiting program is dedicated to attracting, retaining and cultivating talent. Our interns and new graduates work on real projects that affect how our business runs, giving them the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the future of our company. With offices all over the world, our recruits have the chance to collaborate and connect with fellow employees on a global scale. We offer job shadowing, mentorship programs, talent development courses, and much more. **Job Category** Sales **Job Details** **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **Fixed\-Term Contract** **Duration:** 12\-month **Start Date: Aug 2026** The EMEA Graduate Rotation Program is a new and exciting 12 month program which welcomes ambitious graduates who would like to begin their career journey within sales and solution engineering. It is a rotational program designed to give our graduates holistic learning and practical experience across both sales development and solution engineering. Having built the foundational skills and passed all required eligibility and readiness criteria, our graduates can then go on to pursue one of these two clear career paths. Our Top Graduates can potentially promote to the career path of their choice, (depending on business demand) by month 7 of the program. Our graduate program is a fully immersive program combining comprehensive learning paths, in\-role experience, customer interaction and mentorship from senior sales and solution engineering professionals. At Salesforce, you will have the opportunity to lay the foundations for a successful career in sales \& solution engineering and accelerate your career at a fast pace. We are a proud member of *Fortune 100* *Best Places to Work* list for the past 12 years and a consistent winner of *Great Places to Work.* ****Who are we looking for?**** * You are fluent in English \& Dutch * You are a 2024 or 2025 graduate (minimum bachelor’s degree). We consider applicants from All Academic Fields * You are interested in the impact technology can have on the world and business * You are a creative problem\-solver * You are a relationship builder and possess a growth mindset * Are curious, self\-motivated and focused on continuous learning * You thrive working in a team environment * You have excellent interpersonal skills * You are goal\-oriented and comfortable with a fast\-paced environment **What Does Our New EMEA Graduate Program Look Like** The Program is structured as follows: **Introduction Month** During your first month you will have extensive onboarding enablement in a Salesforce\-like academy, where you will: * Get immersed in the culture of salesforce and truly understand our values * Develop an understanding of our solutions, technologies and tools. * Learn critical professional skills for your career in salesforce * Structured learning calendar with sessions \& hands\-on workshops led by experts and program alumni **Rotations** During this Graduate Program you will have the opportunity to experience **the Sales Graduate Path** and **the Solution Engineering Graduate Path.** The structure for both are similar, for both you will spend 60% of your time immersed in the role, you will have clear tasks, clear targets and you will align with key stakeholders and the other 40% of your time will be spent on role specific learning. You will be paired with mentors/buddies with experience in the path. If during your first rotation (months 2 throughout to month 4\) are on the Solution Engineering Graduate Rotation, then you will join the Sales Graduate Rotation (from month 5 through to 7\) and vice versa. So in the these 6 months you will have exposure to both roles. Your time spent mastering the fundamentals of both these roles during these rotations will empower you to be even more productive and impactful when you eventually promote. Throughout the rotations there will be self paced certification and skills assessment. You will be required to meet all readiness eligibility criteria before you can Panel for Promotion. **An Example of what you can expect during the specific rotations** **The Sales Graduate Rotation** * You will be taught salesforce’s best in class prospecting and qualification methodologies * You will learn how to use social, digital, video, chat and phone platforms to effectively engage with Salesforce prospects * You will collaborative with Sales Development Specialists to run campaigns and help build sales pipeline for the EMEA Sales organisation **The Solution Engineer Graduate Rotation** * You will have the opportunity to learn our solutions at a deeper more technical level * You work closely with the solution engineering organisation supporting them in building cutting edge cutomer demonstrations * You will learn the art of customer discovery \& how to create a customer point of view ****What’s in it for you?**** * A clear and structured career path within our Sales and Solution Engineering organisation * Opportunity to work in an international team, within a nurturing environment * Opportunity to join our diverse internal equality groups * Comprehensive benefits package * Relocation budget and guidance in your relocation\-process * Additional seven days paid time off for charitable causes \- check out our 1/1/1 model ****Map Your Future**** With our Graduate Program there are two optional career paths * The Sales Path where after your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Sales Development Representative, Business Development Representative and then into our Sales organisation as Account Executive, where you will position our solutions to potential customers. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * The Solution Engineering path where after your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Associate Solution Engineer and rise to work at every customer level ESB, SMB, GRB. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * With our strong culture of mentoring we strive to develop industry\-leading sales and solution engineering leaders. This is a phenomenal opportunity to develop your international experience in an organisation that has transformed the Enterprise Software Market with Cloud Computing ****Salesforce in Dublin**** When we opened our first office in Ireland in 2000 we were one of the first digital companies to locate here. Today, our Dublin office has grown into a leading technology hub, and Salesforce is the global leader in customer relationship management (CRM), bringing companies of every size and industry together with their customers. Our new Salesforce Tower Dublin will bring our dynamic, multinational team together in the vibrant Silicon Docks area. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. ********Posting Statement******** Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **Fixed\-term contract** **Duration:** 12 months **Start Date:** August 2026 We are thrilled to invite ambitious graduate candidates who would like to build a career in **Salesforce** to apply for the **5th cohort** of our **EMEA Graduate Rotation Program – Solution Engineering Path** . This exciting **12\-month program** is designed to give graduates exposure to both solution engineering and sales. Having built the foundational skills for both roles and met our eligibility criteria, top graduates on this path can begin their **Salesforce Solution Engineering** careers. The promotion window opens from **month 7 onwards.** Our graduate program is a fully immersive experience, combining comprehensive learning paths with in\-role experience. You will be assigned an experienced **Solution Engineering Buddy** who will provide hands\-on coaching and involve you in some of their live deals. You will also be paired with experienced **Sales Development Buddies** during the Sales Rotation and you also get to call your first salesforce leads and find your first deals. Throughout the programme, you will gain exposure to senior solution engineering and sales professionals during our inspiration sessions. By joining Salesforce, you will have the opportunity to lay the foundation for a **fast\-paced, successful career** . We are a proud member of the **Fortune 100 Best Places to Work** list for the past 14 years and a consistent winner of **Great Place to Work** awards. Who are we looking for? * You are a 2025 or 2026 graduate (minimum bachelor’s degree) * For the Solution Engineering Path, we consider applicants from the following academic backgrounds: Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Information Technology, Software Engineering, Data Science/Data Engineering, Information Systems, or other adjacent disciplines * You are a creative problem\-solver * You are a relationship builder and possess a growth mindset * You are curious, self\-motivated, and focused on continuous learning * You thrive in a team environment * You have excellent interpersonal skills * You are goal\-oriented and comfortable in a fast\-paced environment * You are fluent in English and Swedish What does our new EMEA Graduate Program look like? **The Program Is Structured As Follows** Introduction Month During your first month, you will receive extensive onboarding enablement through a Salesforce\-style academy, where you will: * Become immersed in the culture of Salesforce and gain a deep understanding of our values * Develop an understanding of our solutions, technologies, and tools * Learn critical professional skills for your career at Salesforce * Follow a structured learning calendar with sessions and hands\-on workshops led by experts and program alumni Rotations During the Graduate Program, you will experience both the **Solution Engineering Path** and the **Sales Path** . The structure of each rotation is similar: you will spend **60% of your time** immersed in the role with clear tasks, targets, and stakeholder alignment, while the remaining **40%** will focus on role\-specific learning. You will be paired with experienced buddies in each path. If your first rotation (months 2 through 4\) is in **Solution Engineering** , you will then join the **Sales Graduate Rotation** from months 5 through 7, and vice versa. Over these six months, you will gain exposure to both roles. The time spent mastering the fundamentals of these complementary disciplines will enable you to be more productive and impactful upon promotion. Throughout the rotations, there will be **self\-paced certifications and skills assessments** . You must meet all eligibility criteria before you can **panel for promotion** . Examples of what you can expect during the rotations Solution Engineering Graduate Rotation * Learn our solutions at a deeper, more technical level * Work closely with the Solution Engineering organization, supporting the creation of cutting\-edge customer demonstrations * Learn the art of customer discovery and how to create a compelling customer point of view Sales Graduate Rotation * Learn Salesforce’s best\-in\-class prospecting and qualification methodologies * Learn how to use social, digital, video, chat, and phone platforms to effectively engage Salesforce prospects * Collaborate with Sales Development Specialists to run campaigns and help build sales pipeline for the EMEA Sales organization What’s in it for you? * A clear and structured career path within our Solution Engineering organization * The opportunity to work in an international team within a nurturing environment * Access to our diverse internal employee groups * A comprehensive benefits package * Relocation budget and guidance throughout your relocation process * An additional seven days of paid time off for charitable causes – learn more about our 1\-1\-1 model Map your future for the coming years With the **EMEA Graduate Program – Solution Engineering Path** , you can continue to progress: * After completing the Graduate Program rotations, you may progress to Associate Solution Engineer, with opportunities to work across ESB, SMB, and GRB customer segments, or join our Specialist SE team. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * With our strong mentoring culture, we strive to develop industry\-leading Solution Engineering leaders. This is a phenomenal opportunity to build international experience within an organisation that has transformed the enterprise software market through cloud computing. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you require assistance due to a disability applying for open positions please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. ********Posting Statement******** Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Compliance Administrator
description: **Akeso Healthsearch (formerly AGC Healthcare) is seeking to recruit a Compliance Administrator.** Akeso Healthsearch is a leading provider of healthcare recruitment services in Ireland connecting healthcare professionals with public and private clients for permanent placements, contracts and locum. We focus on helping healthcare professionals and providers thrive, as people are our purpose. This is a fantastic opportunity to join a fast\-growing team with excellent career progression and development prospects. Our company has adapted to the hybrid working model, which gives our employees the combination of onsite and offsite work. The Compliance Administrator has a key role in ensuring that the compliance of our healthcare professionals are completed in line with the legislation and HIQA standards. **Requirements:** * Ideally have experience in a similar role. * In\-depth knowledge of employment legislations, immigration law and relevant legislation in the areas outlined below. * Attention to detail is a pre\-requisite * Ability to work on your own initiative * Strong administration skills * Excellent communication and teamwork skills * Strong interpersonal skill * Ability to prioritize your workload * Sense of urgency and ability to thrive in a fast\-paced environment * Self\-motivated and driven **Responsibilities:** * Maintaining appropriate compliance manuals, policies and procedures that meet regulatory requirements. * Manage garda vetting and compliance of Healthcare Professionals. * Maintain accurate records in line with legislation and HIQA standards. * Assisting Healthcare Professionals registering with the required regulatory bodies. * Assisting Healthcare Professionals with their application for validation and recognition of non\-Irish qualification. * Processing of employment visas and work permits. * Reference checks Akeso Healthsearch is an equal opportunities employer and offers an excellent remuneration package for the successful candidate.

title: Computer/STEM teacher
description: **Job Description: Computing/STEM Teacher** Rathdown Junior School is seeking an enthusiastic and innovative Computing/STEM teacher to join our Junior School team. The successful candidate will inspire curiosity, creativity, and confidence in technology and science through engaging, age\-appropriate lessons for primary students. This role combines the teaching of Computing to students from 2nd–6th Class and STEM/Science to students from Junior Infants–2nd Class, while also supporting the effective use of technology throughout the Junior School. **Key Responsibilities:** **1\. Computing** **Aim \-\-** To ensure all children are computer literate by 6th Class, both in the computer lab and online. **Teaching Responsibilities \-\-** \* Deliver 40\-minute computing lessons to students from 2nd–6th Class \* Teach approximately 14 computing classes per week \* Plan and deliver engaging, differentiated lessons that develop digital literacy and computational thinking \* Support and collaborate with class teachers on digital learning projects \* Monitor student progress and contribute to school reports **Areas of Instruction** **\-\-** Examples of programmes and skills taught include: \* Microsoft PowerPoint / Google Slides \* Microsoft Word / Google Docs \* Spreadsheets / Google Sheets \* Coding Town \* GameMaker \* Kodable \* Video editing and digital creativity tools **Technical \& Staff Support \-\-** \* Assist teachers with classroom technology and IT\-related matters \* Liaise with GPS IT Services regarding: o Computers and Chromebooks o WiFi connectivity o Printing and technical issues \* Provide in\-class support to teachers when required **Key Responsibilities:** **2\. STEM / Science** **Aim \-\-** In line with the new Irish Primary Science Curriculum, the aim of the programme is to foster curiosity, develop an evidence\-based understanding of the natural world, and cultivate scientific literacy through inquiry\-based learning. **Teaching Responsibilkities \-\-** \* Deliver 40\-minute STEM/Science lessons to students from Junior Infants–2nd Class \* Teach approximately 4–6 STEM classes per week \* Create engaging, hands\-on learning experiences that encourage exploration and problem\-solving \* Support the development of scientific thinking and environmental awareness inyoung learners **Curriculum Strands \-\-** Lessons will focus on: \* Living Things \* Energy and Forces \* Materials \* Environmental Awareness and Care **The ideal candidate will:** \* Have experience teaching primary\-aged children \* Demonstrate strong digital literacy and confidence using educational technology \* Be enthusiastic about STEM and inquiry\-based learning \* Possess excellent organisational and communication skills \* Be collaborative, flexible, and proactive in supporting staff and students \* Have the ability to troubleshoot basic IT issues within a school environment \* Have strong classroom and behaviour management skills \* Have excellent communication and interpersonal skills. \* Have the ability to motivate and engage young children. \* Have organisational and time\-management skills. \* Be creative in lesson planning and activity design. **Desirable:** \* University degree in any STEM subject(s) **Position Details:** \* Total classes per week: approximately 18–20 \* Teaching hours: approximately 12–14 hours per week \* Additional classes and responsibilities may arise from time to time as agreed with the Junior School Principal **Further Benefits:** \* 8% Holiday pay in final payroll \* Statutory sick pay \* CPD and career development opportunities \* EPV days \* Canteen with hot food **How to Apply:** Please email your CV and a cover letter outlining your relevant experience and suitability for the role to: ddix@rathdownschool.ie. Please note that interviews will be conducted in person onsite at Rathdown Junior School. We are an equal opportunities employer and welcome applications from all suitably qualified candidates, regardless of race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, disability, or age. We look forward to hearing from you. Pay: €30\.00\-€38\.00 per hour Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Contracts Administrator I
description: **What This Job Entails** The Contracts Administrator I will ensure all contracts are drafted, reviewed and executed both on the vendor and client side in a timely and accurate manner. The successful associate will also perform key quality reviews to reduce risk working in conjunction with other teams. **Scope** * Applies company policies and procedures to resolve routine issues * Works on problems of limited scope * Receives detailed instructions **Your Roles And Responsibilities** * Assist in preparation and revision of contracts that involve the purchase of sale of goods and services including the drafting, review and execution of contracts according to client policies. + Update and maintain internal database with pertinent information, including legal documents, abstractions, and executive summaries. + Assist in proposal planning and administration of contracts. + Prepare contract briefs and revisions summarizing contractual requirements and budgets. + Track authorizations and correspondence, maintaining detailed and organized files. + Prepare contract change notices + Maintain an audit file for each contract which will include original contract, all correspondence, changes/deviations, amendments, clarifications, payment schedules. + Perform closing activities as needed. + Analyze and mitigate risk. * Other duties as required. title: Corporate Banking Associate

Citi has a 20% market share of all episodic financing and advisory transactions since 2020 and has recently been awarded Euromoney’s Best Investment Bank in Ireland award for the 7th consecutive year. The overall objective of this role is to drive revenue by cross selling the Citi platform while efficiently deploying the firm's capital and evaluating and managing risk. **Responsibilities:** * Support senior bankers to originate opportunities with existing clients form the corporate, financial institutions and public sector industries, working closely with colleagues from Investment Banking, Capital Markets, Treasury \& Trade Solutions and Markets * Support Bankers in cross\-selling full spectrum of products and services by identifying customer needs to generate revenue * Produce high quality client\-facing analytical material on topics including corporate finance, capital structure and optimal financing strategy * Responsible for evaluating public filings, research reports, operations, business plans, and financial forecasts to produce strategic and financial analyses * Responsible for staying abreast of market and industry trends, “best practices” and competitive background and knowledge of Citi’s credit policies, * Consistently demonstrates clear and concise written and verbal communication skills * Ideally 2\-6 years of relevant experience in Corporate Banking, Investment Banking, Credit, Leveraged Finance, or Commercial Banking **Education:** * Bachelor’s/University degree or equivalent experience *This Role is a “Controlled Function” Role as title: Corporate Credit Portfolio Monitoring Analyst/ Associate
description: **Company Overview** KKR is a leading global investment firm that offers alternative asset management as well as capital markets and insurance solutions. KKR aims to generate attractive investment returns by following a patient and disciplined investment approach, employing world\-class people, and supporting growth in its portfolio companies and communities. KKR sponsors investment funds that invest in private equity, credit and real assets and has strategic partners that manage hedge funds. KKR’s insurance subsidiaries offer retirement, life and reinsurance products under the management of Global Atlantic Financial Group. References to KKR’s investments may include the activities of its sponsored funds and insurance subsidiaries. KKR is hiring an Analyst/Associate in its growing Private Credit Corporate business in Dublin. KKR is one of the world’s largest and most successful investment firms with a 50\-year track record of investment excellence and now managing $700bn\+ globally across public and private markets. To enhance our ability to actively manage and optimize investments, KKR established the Portfolio Monitoring Unit (PMU) in Dublin in 2016\. This dedicated team plays a pivotal role in overseeing our global Private Credit portfolio, ensuring disciplined risk management and long\-term value creation. **Key Areas Of Focus** * Financial \& Performance Analysis – Conducting in\-depth evaluations of company financials to assess trends * Asset Valuation \& Financial Modelling – Applying advanced methodologies to ensure accurate valuations. * Portfolio \& Risk Management – Identifying and mitigating risks across a diverse set of investments. Since its inception, the Corporate PMU team has grown to 19 investment professionals, now responsible for monitoring 165 investments across Europe, the U.S., and APAC. These investments span a wide range of industries, including technology, healthcare, industrials, and consumer goods. Through proactive portfolio management and data\-driven decision\-making, we continue to enhance performance and drive sustainable growth across our global credit investments. We are seeking a highly analytical and detail\-oriented investment professional to join our portfolio management team. This role offers the opportunity to work closely with senior leadership, assess portfolio company performance, and contribute to strategic decision\-making. **Key Responsibilities** * Portfolio Performance Analysis – Conduct detailed financial statement evaluations to assess underlying company performance. * Risk Assessment – Identify and analyse financial and commercial risks, providing insights into overall portfolio exposure. * Financial Modelling \& Valuation – Develop financial models, perform sensitivity analyses, and conduct ongoing valuation assessments, including Discounted Cash Flow, Relative Value, and Enterprise Value Waterfall analysis. * Investment Review \& Reporting – Prepare comprehensive quarterly reviews and present key findings to the Investment Committee. * Stakeholder Engagement – Maintain direct communication with senior leadership of portfolio companies, including CEOs and CFOs. * Strategic Collaboration – Work alongside origination teams on refinancing, acquisition financing, restructurings, and exit strategies. **Candidate Profile** * Experience: 2\-5 years in investment banking, private equity, corporate or leveraged lending, corporate finance, transaction services, restructuring, corporate audit, or valuations. * Analytical Acumen: Strong problem\-solving skills and a keen eye for financial analysis. * Academic Excellence: A solid educational background in finance, accounting, economics, or a related field. * Commercial Awareness: A deep understanding of financial statements, valuation methodologies, and corporate structures. * Communication \& Organizational Skills: Ability to synthesize complex financial data into clear, actionable insights. This role presents a unique opportunity to contribute meaningfully to a high\-performing investment team. If you have the expertise and ambition to excel in a dynamic environment, we encourage you to apply. KKR is an equal opportunity employer. Individuals seeking employment are considered without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, sexual orientation, or any other category protected by applicable law. KKR will provide reasonable accommodations as required by applicable federal, state, and/or local laws. Individuals seeking an accommodation for the application or interview process should email Benefits@kkr.com. Emails sent for unrelated issues, such as following up on an application, will not receive a response. If you are a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran, you may request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access https://www.kkr.com/careers because of your disability. You can request reasonable accommodations by sending an email to Benefits@kkr.com. Only emails left for this purpose will be returned. **Massachusetts Applicants:** It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability. This notice applies only to applicants and employees who work or will work in Massachusetts, in accordance with applicable state law.

title: Counter Associate - The Ordinary - Brown Thomas, Dublin - PT

Access to Development Grants \& a LinkedIn Learning membership to help you keep growing and learning. First dibs on new products. These values continue to guide us in all that we do. Along the way, we’ve been honoured to win some pretty incredible awards, including a CEW Achiever Award, Best Skincare Brand at Sunday Times Style Beauty Awards, and Most Popular Sustainable Brand by The Cosmetify Index Global. We’re still blushing! **SUSTAINABILITY AND SOCIAL IMPACT** DECIEM’s objective is to build growth and do good things while prioritizing people, animals, and the Earth. Our Sustainability strategy is focused on fighting climate change, improved waste management, responsible packaging, responsible sourcing and product lifecycle, and water conservation. In 2020, DECIEM established the Good Fund to support both large and grassroots charities in response to global events. **CREATING A HUMAN WORLD OF BEAUTY** DECIEM is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) by creating meaningful, measurable change in the lives of our team members and in the communities where we operate. title: Customer Assistant (Permanent 25 - 30 Hours) -  - Lucan
description: **About the role** We pride ourselves on offering customers the biggest range of products around. And that's not just food, but everything from clothes and kettles to financial services. Through initiatives like the Tesco Community Fund, our association with Childrenâ€™s Health Foundation, our partnership with FoodCloud and our Stronger Starts programme, we have supported over 21,000 community groups, raised over â‚¬7\.5 million for medical equipment, and provided almost 15 million meals nationwide, to those living in food insecurity. Tesco is the single largest buyer of Irish food and drink in the world, buying â‚¬1\.6 Billion a year; more than any other single country in the European Union, more than even the USA which you perceive as a massive buyer of Irish food. Â We work closely with suppliers across Ireland who are dedicated to making the best products for our customers and this is reflected in the numerous awards our products have won over the years including BlÃ¡s Na hÃ‰ireann, World Steak Challenge and Great Taste Awards. We are proud to have an inclusive culture at Tesco where everyone truly feels able to be themselves. It is embedded in our values: we treat people how they want to be treated. Â At Tesco, we not only celebrate diversity, but recognise the value and opportunity it brings. title: Customer Engagement Applications
description: ****Company Overview**** KLA is a global leader in diversified electronics for the semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem. Virtually every electronic device in the world is produced using our technologies. No laptop, smartphone, wearable device, voice\-controlled gadget, flexible screen, VR device or smart car would have made it into your hands without us. KLA invents systems and solutions for the manufacturing of wafers and reticles, integrated circuits, packaging, printed circuit boards and flat panel displays. The innovative ideas and devices that are advancing humanity all begin with inspiration, research and development. KLA focuses more than average on innovation and we invest 15% of sales back into R\&D. Our expert teams of physicists, engineers, data scientists and problem\-solvers work together with the world’s leading technology providers to accelerate the delivery of tomorrow’s electronic devices. Life here is exciting and our teams thrive on tackling really hard problems. There is never a dull moment with us. ****Job Description/Preferred Qualifications**** **Applications Engineer – LS/SWIFT Division** The LS\-SWIFT Division of KLA’s Global Products Group provides patterned wafer inspection systems for high\-volume semiconductor manufacturing. Its mission is to deliver market\-leading cost of ownership in defect detection for a broad range of applications in the production of semiconductors. Customers from the foundry, logic, memory, automotive, MEMS, advanced packaging and other markets rely upon high\-sample wafer inspection information generated by LS\-SWIFT products. LS (Laser Scanning) systems enable cost\-effective patterned wafer defect detection for the industry’s most sophisticated process technologies deployed in leading\-edge foundry, logic, DRAM, and NAND fabs. SWIFT (Simultaneous Wafer Inspection at Fast Throughput) systems deliver all\-wafer\-surface (frontside, backside, and edge) macro inspection that is critical for automotive IC, MEMS, and advanced packaging processes as well as foundry/logic and memory fabs. As an Applications Engineer, you’ll work closely with customers and multi\-functional teams to maintain and optimize laser scan tools, develop innovative applications, and drive improvements for next\-generation products. This role combines technical expertise with customer engagement, ensuring our tools deliver maximum value in advanced semiconductor fabs. ****Job Description/Preferred Qualifications**** * Deliver expert technical support that ensures optimal tool performance in customer fabs * Implement detailed installation plans * Develop and implement applications that solve customer problems using existing and pre\-release products * Work with engineering teams to resolve hardware/software issues, scope feature requests to improve current products, and collect requirements for future products * Coordinate activities with customers and internal teams * Maintain strategic customer relationships * Deliver technical presentations and customer training * Assist sales teams with technical assessments and product specifications * Travel Required up to 20% to support high\-volume manufacturing sites ****Qualifications**** * Doctorate (Academic) Degree and 0 years related work experience; Master's Level Degree and related work experience of 3 years; Bachelor's Level Degree and related work experience of 5 years * Experience in semiconductor fabrication or equivalent educational experience * Strong knowledge of semiconductor process flows and defect types * Experience in system\-level troubleshooting, diagnostics, and reporting * Cleanroom experience and semiconductor device characterization * Familiarity with computer operating systems, networking, and analysis tools **Preferred Skills:** * Strong customer focus and ability to build collaborative relationships * Motivation to innovate and proactively deliver exceptional customer outcomes * Proven technical leadership and problem\-solving skills * Excellent communication, teamwork, and project management skills * Ability to present technical content to diverse audiences * Data analysis tools (Excel, JMP, Minitab, Igor, Origin, ImageJ, etc.) ****Minimum Qualifications**** Doctorate (Academic) Degree and 0 years related work experience; Master's Level Degree and related work experience of 3 years; Bachelor's Level Degree and related work experience of 5 years *We offer a competitive, family friendly total rewards package. We design our programs to reflect our commitment to an inclusive environment, while ensuring we provide benefits that meet the diverse needs of our employees.* *KLA is proud to be an equal opportunity employer* Be aware of potentially fraudulent job postings or suspicious recruiting activity by persons that are currently posing as KLA employees. title: Customer Growth Associate, Early Careers, Google Customer Solutions (Dutch Market)
description: **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience. * Experience in digital sales, client management, customer service, business development, customer outreach, marketing, consulting, digital advertising, or related field. * Ability to communicate in English and Dutch fluently to engage with clients in the region. **Preferred qualifications:** * Familiarity with Google Ads and online advertising, with Google Digital Workshop certification or equivalent experience. * Ability to build compelling narratives and utilize storytelling as a client engagement strategy. * Ability to influence stakeholders internally and externally to deliver impact. * Ability to own an advertising or media sales portfolio in the Netherlands, and consistently overachieve quotas to drive growth. **About The Job** Businesses of all shapes and sizes rely on Google’s unparalleled advertising solutions to help them grow in today's dynamic marketing environment. As a Customer Growth Associate, you bring a passion for sales, knowledge of online media, and commitment to maximize customer success. Building trusted relationships with customers along the way, you'll uncover their business needs and translate them into powerful solutions to achieve their most ambitious goals. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. title: Customer Service Representative
description: **Customer Service Representative – Motor Industry** Noel Group are currently recruiting for a Customer Service Representative on behalf of our well\-established client based in Park West, Dublin.
