title: Inspector Grade III - Chemical/Product Compliance
description: **About the Role** We have an opening for an Inspector Grade III level within our Chemical regulatory and Competent Authority mandate. The role involves monitoring and enforcing compliance with the Chemicals Acts and relevant EU chemicals legislation, including participation in and support of EU wide enforcement and chemical products market surveillance campaigns. The Inspector will be working as part of a dynamic team and contributing to the performance of Competent Authority functions under EU Regulations. This role may also require some EU travel. The following is a non\-exhaustive list of key accountabilities that are typically allocated, but are not limited to, the role: **Key Responsibilities** * Contributing to the Authorities Competent Authority chemical inspection related functions * Carrying out and recording inspections and product assessments up to and including enforcement action and the issuing of enforcement notices as appropriate * Undertaking risk assessments for high hazard chemical products * Ensuring the accurate and timely entering of information and data onto the relevant information technology platforms * Developing, maintaining and providing technical expertise related to chemicals regulated by the Authority * Participation in European activities of the European Chemical Agency’s Forum on Enforcement * Supporting awareness raising activities, engaging with relevant stakeholders and representing the Authority at meetings with stakeholders * Contributing to the development of guidance, policy and procedures * Researching technical queries to support the activities including responding to queries to the Authority’s Chemical Helpdesk and contact centre * Participation in team meetings and contributing to development of policies and procedures activities when required **Essential Requirements** The ideal candidate must be able to demonstrate they meet the competencies as set out in the Appendix and also the following essential requirements: * Primary degree (NFQ level 8 or equivalent) in Science, engineering or other appropriate qualification relevant to the role * Minimum of two years relevant experience in a regulatory, industry or a post graduate setting * Possession of a full driving licence (Category B). **Desirable Requirements** In addition to the above essential requirements, it would be a decided advantage for candidates to have: * Inspection experience or familiarity with chemical and/or product regulation Successful candidates will be placed on a panel from which appointments will be made. Vacancies will be offered to candidates placed on the relevant panel based on the order of merit from the interview process. Any panel formed as part of this campaign, will expire 12 months from its establishment. **Location** This role can be based in the Authority’s Dublin HQ or in one of the Authority’s regional offices (Athlone, Cork, Galway, Kilkenny, Limerick, Sligo and Waterford) as may be agreed by the Authority and the candidate. Candidates will be asked to indicate their preferred location(s) as part of the application process. **How to Apply:** Conscia will be managing all aspects of the recruitment process on behalf of the Health and Safety Authority. Applications must be made by attaching a **completed application form via the Conscia website.** Only applications fully submitted online will be accepted into the campaign. Applications will not be accepted after the closing date of **Thursday 4th of June at 5pm.**

title: Internship Opportunities 2026
description: **At Cadence, we hire and develop leaders and innovators who want to make an impact on the world of technology.** Cadence is a pivotal leader in electronic design, building upon more than 30 years of computational software expertise. The company applies its underlying Intelligent System Design strategy to deliver software, hardware and IP that turn design concepts into reality. Cadence customers are the world’s most innovative companies, delivering extraordinary electronic products from chips to boards to systems for the most dynamic market applications including consumer, hyperscale computing, 5G communications, automotive, aerospace industrial and health. Cadence also offer everything else you would expect from a world\-class technology organization and a Fortune 100 company, which is ranked as one of the best places to work worldwide: https://fortune.com/best\-companies/2020/cadence Cadence Cork are recruiting key talent across several Universities to join our Internship Programme for 2026\. If you are passionate about technology and would love to learn and grow in a cross\-geographic and multi\-cultural organization, developing next\-generation software, then Cadence Cork is for you. As part of your experience with Cadence, you will join a new class of professionals in our Career Catalyst Program. Through this program, you’ll receive special opportunities to develop your leadership capabilities, learn from Cadence executives and other industry leaders, and socialize and network with your peers. ***At Cadence, we hire and develop leaders and innovators who want to make an impact on the world of technology.*** **Job Title: Intern Opportunities 2026 (Software / Hardware Design)** **Location: Cork/Galway/Dublin** **Job Overview** We aim to make you an integral part of the team by providing training, mentorship, and encouragement of your creative talents. Our goal is to equip students with practical work experience. We understand that it is best to learn by doing. We will provide tasks that enable you to learn while making meaningful technical contributions. As part of the Cadence Internship program, you will get the opportunity to support different Business Units across the following functions including; * Digital Design Signoff * Customer IC / Analog / RF Design * System Analysis – including Thermal Design Management, EM solvers ad CFD Analysis * PCB Design and Analysis * System Design and Verification **The wealth of knowledge, experience, learning and autonomous culture of the team makes it a great team to work with.** **Job Qualifications** * Currently enrolled as BS or MS or Phd student majoring in Mathematics, Physics, Computer Science / Electrical / Electronic Engineering / Digital Humanities \& Information Technology or similar. * Looking for strong C\+\+, algorithms, and software development / scripting skills. * Understanding of analog/RF/custom IC design and verification practices is a strong plus. * Exposure to Cadence Virtuoso Layout and physical verification tools is a plus. * Team player, driven, self\-motivated and autonomous. * Excellent communication, presentation and customer service skills. * Problem Solving skills. **Additional Information** Cadence is committed to equal employment opportunity and employment equity throughout all levels of the organization. We strive to attract a qualified and diverse candidate pool and encourage diversity and inclusion in the workplace. **We’re doing work that matters. Help us solve what others can’t.**

title: Investment Analyst, Ireland
description: **Investment Analyst, Ireland** **About Federated Hermes:** Federated Hermes, Inc. is a global leader in active, responsible investment management, with $907B in assets under management, as of March 31, 2026\. We deliver investment solutions that help investors target a broad range of outcomes and provide equity, fixed\-income, alternative/private markets, multi asset, and liquidity management strategies to more than 11,000 institutions and intermediaries worldwide. Our clients include corporations, government entities, insurance companies, foundations and endowments, banks, and broker/dealers. Headquartered in Pittsburgh, Federated Hermes has more than 2,000 employees in London, New York and offices worldwide. For more information, visit FederatedHermes.com **Main Function of the role:** Hermes Fund Managers Ireland Limited (“ **HFMIL** ”) is a subsidiary of Federated Hermes Limited (“ **FHL** ”). The role of the Investment Analyst is to provide support for the Head of Investment/ Designated Person for Investment Management in the HFMIL Dublin office. A core element of their role will be to provide insight and interrogation of the various investment products which are managed by HFMIL, including public markets, private equity, private credit, money market funds and trade finance asset types. **Key task and responsibilities:** * Conduct detailed portfolio analysis to assess investment exposures, evaluating asset allocations, investment risk concentrations, performance trends, and alignment with fund investment strategies. * Interpret investment guidelines through an investment‑lens in light of regulatory and client mandates, alerting the team of any potential breaches to the investment management of the products, reviewing changes to these to ensure they meet regulatory requirement/adapting oversight framework. * Organise and attend calls with portfolio managers to discuss mandates, insights on investment portfolio and the impact of proposed strategies on portfolio risk and guideline constraints. * Analyse market events and regulatory developments on investment thesis, to assess potential impacts on portfolios, investment processes, guidelines and positioning. * Support in oversight of investment decision‑making across asset classes (equities, fixed income, derivatives, alternatives). * Evaluate new investment products and strategies, performing rule‑mapping oversight and scenario hypothesis to understand investment strategy, risk characteristics, and operational implications. * Prepare and deliver investment‑focused reports for senior management, providing clear and actionable insights on exposures, trends and breaches. * Prepare portfolio manager meeting notes and develop and maintain dashboards or information for any investment specific forum, including committees, boards or internal meetings. * Assist with ad hoc projects and initiatives both within the Irish office and Federated Hermes Group offices. - **Experience Required** A strong knowledge of data analysis is a pre\-requisite. This includes analysing investment data, ability to translate guidelines and evaluate investment rules. A knowledge of technology solutions such as Power Bi dashboards, and a working understanding of SQL and python would be considered beneficial. **Minimum requirements/Candidate Profile:** * A confident, articulate individual with strong communication and interpersonal skills * Ability to quickly understand problems, articulate them and offer solutions * Analytical background with the ability to demonstrate insights derived from previous data\-driven projects and to provide examples of value added by these insights * Ability to assimilate information from across the business and quickly build up knowledge * Effective organisation and planning skills with the ability to prioritise across multiple disciplines * Deadline and results focused * Pro\-active, inquisitive approach to actual and potential business activities * Demonstrates personal drive and energy in support of HFMIL’s and individual objectives * A collaborative team player with interpersonal understanding: treats everyone with dignity, honesty and respect **Diversity, Inclusion and Flexible Working** We believe in genuine opportunity, fairness, and respect for all applicants and aim to ensure that no current or prospective employee is subject to discrimination or treated less favourably on the basis of their disability, gender identity or expression, race, ethnicity, religion or belief, marriage or civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, age, sex, or sexual orientation, or disability. We understand that applicants may have different needs and expectations related to their working arrangements and are committed to a discussion on flexible working. Please raise these with your Recruitment contact to discuss what type of arrangements can work for you and the role for which you are applying. We welcome applications from all eligible candidates, and we particularly encourage applications from qualified candidates who identify as women and/or are from a Black, Asian and Minority Ethnic background, as these groups are underrepresented throughout the financial services sector. We are a Disability Confident Employer and participate in the “Offer of Interview” scheme, intended to actively encourage applications from people with a disability or long\-term condition and offer an interview to those who meet the minimum criteria set out within this job description. You will need to let us know if you would like to opt in to this scheme by contacting Jessica Sheehan at Jessica.sheehan@federatedhermes.com or \+44 207 702 0888\. You do not need to share details of your disability or long\-term condition. If you would benefit from alternative methods of applying or for workplace adjustments during the recruitment process, please also let us know by contacting hr@federatedhermes.com to discuss the support you need.

title: JD Young Financial Operations Internship
description: **Who We Are** At JD.com, also known as JINGDONG, everything begins with a bold idea and the drive to make life smarter, faster, and more connected. What started in China has grown into one of the world’s most trusted technology and supply chain ecosystems, serving over 700 million customers. From retail and logistics to tech, property, and beyond, we’re all about innovation that actually makes a difference. **Our Global Reach** JD.com is expanding worldwide through a digitally intelligent, cross\-border supply chain and world\-class logistics network. With platforms like Joybuy connecting trusted products to customers everywhere, our teams stretch across China, the U.S., Europe, APAC, the Middle East, and beyond. Every year, we welcome over 20,000 students and graduates into our business, showing our commitment to young talent and the fresh ideas that drive our future. Join us and contribute to global projects from day one. Learn more about who we are and what we do: https://corporate.jd.com/ **What We Offer** We believe great ideas can come from anywhere, and often, they come from you. As an intern or graduate at JD.com, you’ll work on meaningful, hands\-on projects, learn from talented colleagues across our global network, and grow in a supportive, fast\-paced environment designed for development. With competitive compensation, great office locations, and a people\-first culture that champions continuous learning, you’ll have everything you need to thrive and kick\-start your career. **About Our Program** You’ll be joining the JD Vanguard (JDV) Graduate Program, our frontline leadership pathway for the next generation of supply chain talent. Over the course of a year, you’ll take on real projects, build technical and people\-leadership skills, and learn how JD Logistics operates at a global level, with the opportunity to extend and continue your journey. * Bill Reconciliation and Exception Handling: Review logistics costs, insurance premiums, and other bills, identify data discrepancies, and drive resolution of exceptions to ensure timely collection of receivables, reduce bad debt risk, and improve settlement efficiency and customer satisfaction. * Process Optimization and System Improvement: Analyze pain points in the settlement process, propose system function optimization requirements and drive their implementation, design automation tools to reduce manual intervention, enhance settlement efficiency and accuracy, and support business scalability. * Risk Control and Compliance Management: Monitor contract terms, settlement documents, and fund flows, identify compliance risks, and develop preventive measures to ensure compliance with financial policies and regulatory requirements, safeguarding company funds and maintaining a compliant corporate image. * Cross\-Department Collaboration and Support: Coordinate with business and technical departments to allocate resources, resolve bottlenecks in the settlement process, provide financial training and data support, promote policy implementation, and improve cross\-team collaboration efficiency and business health. * Data Analysis and Decision Support: Regularly produce settlement analysis reports, use data modeling to identify operational risks and optimization opportunities, provide decision\-making support to management, and assist in business strategy adjustments and goal achievement. * Accounts Receivable Management and Bad Debt Monitoring: Track accounts receivable aging and payment progress, develop and implement collection strategies, conduct statistical analysis of bad debt causes, and propose process improvement suggestions to ensure efficient cash flow and financial stability. **About you** * Currently pursuing a degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, Supply Chain, or a related field * Strong analytical skills and comfort working with numbers and data * Interest in financial operations, billing, and process optimization * Basic understanding of Procure\-to\-Pay (P2P) and/or Order\-to\-Cash (O2C) processes is a plus * Proficiency in Excel and Microsoft Office tools * Detail\-oriented, organized, and able to manage multiple tasks simultaneously * Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate across teams * Problem\-solving mindset with the ability to identify process improvement opportunities * English proficiency is required * 6\-month full\-time internship, fully onsite in our Dublin office * ASAP start date preferred, with potential opportunity to convert to a full\-time position based on performance *At JD.com, we are committed to creating an inclusive workplace where every voice counts and every talent belongs. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age, veteran status, or any legally protected characteristic.* **Ready To Apply?** Don't leave yourself wondering 'what if'. Hit apply and see where your curiosity can take you at JD.com

title: Join Sisk – Speculative Applications
description: **Overview** The Sisk Group are always looking for talented and experienced construction professionals to join our growing teams across Sisk Ireland, Vision Built, and Sensori. Even if we do not currently have a specific vacancy that matches your experience, we welcome speculative applications from individuals who may be a great fit for future opportunities. ****Who Should Apply**** This channel is intended for experienced professionals with Tier 1 construction experience. **Please Note** * This is not for graduate, junior, or early\-career candidates. * Only applications submitted through this page will be considered; email submissions cannot be accepted. ****Why Submit a Speculative Application**** As a Leading Tier 1 Construction Contractor, Sisk Has a Diverse And Evolving Project Pipeline. By Submitting Your Profile, You Allow Us To * Keep your details on file for future opportunities that match your skills and experience * Consider you for roles across Sisk Ireland, Vision Built, and Sensori as projects arise Even if a role is not currently advertised, your experience could make you a strong candidate for future positions. ****How to Apply**** * Complete the application form on this page * Include your up\-to\-date CV and a summary of your experience * Ensure your profile reflects senior\-level or Tier 1 construction experience We review all speculative applications carefully and will contact you if a suitable opportunity arises. Thank you for your interest in building your career with Sisk. We look forward to receiving your application. **The Sisk Talent Team** **Additional Information** * Competitive salary * 26 days annual leave * Pension and benefits * Unlimited learning and development opportunities * Much more **Our People and Recruitment Commitment** At Sisk Group our people are at the heart of our success. We offer the chance to work with purpose, build a career with no limits and be part of a great team. All vacancies are managed directly by our Internal Recruitment Team. Unsolicited contact or speculative CVs from unapproved agencies will not be accepted and no introductory fees will apply. We are an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, race, ethnicity, disability, age, sexual orientation, religion or any other protected characteristic. Reasonable adjustments are available during the recruitment process. We know the confidence gap and imposter phenomenon can stop talented candidates applying. You do not need to meet every criterion – your skills and potential matter. Don’t hold back we want to hear from you. All recruitment materials comply with legal and regulatory requirements including the EU Pay Transparency Directive and the Equality Acts UK and Ireland.

title: Junior Accountant
description: **About Lazer Hits** Lazer Hits is a dedicated radio station broadcasting across Leinster on DAB\+, as well as online, via Alexa, and through our dedicated app. We are a station built on a simple promise: **"Never more than a minute away from the music."** We specialize in the greatest hits from the 60s to the early 90s. We don't do news, we don’t do ads, and we definitely don’t do jargon. Our philosophy is **"Maximum music and no BS."** As we expand our footprint in the Irish broadcasting landscape, we are looking for a Junior Accountant who shares our straightforward, high\-energy approach to join our growing team. **The Role** This is a unique entry\-level opportunity for a motivated individual to gain hands\-on experience in the media industry. You won’t just be crunching numbers; you will be an integral part of a growing station’s support structure. Working under the direct mentorship and guidance of our Senior Accountant, you will receive professional training and insight into the financial management of a digital, innovating and progressive radio station. **Key Responsibilities** * **Daily Bookkeeping:** Assisting with the maintenance of financial records and ledgers. * **Processing Transactions:** Managing invoices, tracking expenses, and assisting with bank reconciliations. * **Financial Administration:** Supporting the Senior Accountant with month\-end procedures and reporting. * **Documentation:** Ensuring all financial filing and records are organized and compliant. * **Team Support:** Providing general administrative assistance to the finance department as the station expands. **Requirements** * **Ambition to Learn:** You should be currently pursuing an accounting qualification (ATI, ACCA, CIMA, CPA) or be a recent graduate looking for your first professional break. * **Numerical Accuracy:** Exceptional attention to detail and a commitment to precision. * **Software Skills:** Proficiency in Microsoft Excel; prior exposure to accounting software is a plus but training will be provided. * **Professionalism:** Ability to handle sensitive financial information with total discretion. * **Communication:** A friendly, professional manner and the ability to work well within a close\-knit team. **What We Offer** * **Expert Mentorship:** Learn the ropes from a highly experienced Senior Accountant. * **Career Foundation:** Build your CV within the fast\-paced, exciting environment of a new radio brand. * **Flexibility:** A minimum 16\-hour weekly commitment, with the potential for schedule flexibility. * **Future Growth:** As Lazer Hits grows, we want our team to grow with us. **To Apply** Please submit your CV and a short cover letter. We are looking for someone who is ready to dive in and learn the business of broadcasting from the ground up. *Lazer Hits is an equal opportunity employer.* Pay: From €33,000\.00 per year Benefits: * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Junior CAMO Engineer
description: **Reporting Structure** Reports directly to the Fleet Principal Engineer (TS\-S), or by individual appointment to the appointed CAMO Engineer (TS\-E). The TS\-J role exists to support the TS\-S or TS\-E in ensuring all aircraft type\-related continuing airworthiness activities are carried out. Responsibility for workload distribution sits with the TS\-S, while TS\-J remains accountable for the quality of their own duties and for flagging any suspected shortfalls in oversight or resourcing. **Duties and Responsibilities** * Maintain awareness of the airworthiness status of each aircraft in the managed fleet, ensuring all operational and emergency equipment required for flight is serviceable * Liaise with aircraft, engine, and component manufacturers, maintenance organisations, and the Flight Operations Division * Coordinate MEL and PFI activities in line with aircraft ferry and delivery requirements * Assist in the development, amendment, and control of the Aircraft Maintenance Programme * Support airworthiness records management and maintenance data control * Assist in the management of Airworthiness Directives, Service Bulletins, modifications, and repairs * Contribute reports, statistics, and analysis to the Reliability Programme and support evaluation of maintenance programme effectiveness * Support planning of all maintenance activities, including scheduled and unscheduled base maintenance and major asset visits * Support control and monitoring of aircraft, engine, landing gear, life\-limited parts (LLP), and hard\-time (HT) components * Monitor and schedule non\-routine and deferred maintenance activities, including MEL/CDL requirements * Maintain accurate and up\-to\-date records in the AMOS IT system * Support the accomplishment of operational directives, airworthiness directives, and all continued airworthiness requirements, including CRMs, LLPs, airworthiness limitations, CDCCL requirements, and approved modifications and repairs * Issue clear and accurate work orders to maintenance contractors * Participate in material planning relevant to the maintenance activity * Carry out any additional duties within the TS\-J functional area as directed by the TS\-S or TS\-E

title: Junior Ecologist
description: The Junior Ecologist supports the delivery of ecological surveys, assessments, and reporting across a wide range of infrastructure, energy, renewables, and development projects. The role provides an opportunity to develop core consultancy skills while working as part of multidisciplinary project teams under the guidance of senior and principal ecologists. **Key Responsibilities** **Field Survey Support** * Assist with ecological field surveys including habitat surveys, protected species surveys, and monitoring work (e.g. GCN, bats, birds, otter, water vole – depending on experience and season). * Support data collection, recording, and interpretation in line with recognised survey guidance. * Follow health, safety, and biosecurity procedures during fieldwork. **Assessment \& Reporting** * Support the preparation of technical reports, including: + Ecological Impact Assessments (EcIA) + EIA ecology chapters + Baseline reports and survey summaries * Assist with data analysis, mapping, and presentation of findings. * Contribute to mitigation measures and recommendations under supervision. **Project Support** * Work within project teams to deliver ecology inputs to agreed scope, programme, and budget. * Assist with project administration, scheduling surveys, and coordination with colleagues. * Support senior staff in responding to client queries and regulator requests. **Learning \& Development** * Develop technical knowledge of UK wildlife legislation, planning policy, and best‑practice guidance. * Participate in training, mentoring, and technical development activities. * Build competency towards independent survey and reporting capability. **Collaboration \& Culture** * Work collaboratively with ecologists, EIA practitioners, planners, and other specialists. * Contribute positively to team culture and knowledge sharing. * Uphold ERM values, quality standards, and commitment to sustainability. **Essential** **Skills \& Experience** * Degree in Ecology, Environmental Science, or a related discipline. * Basic understanding of UK ecology, habitats, and wildlife legislation. * Willingness to undertake fieldwork, including early starts and occasional travel. * Strong attention to detail and good written communication skills. * Ability to work effectively as part of a team. * Full UK driving licence (or working towards). **Desirable** * Some experience of ecological field surveys (e.g. through placements, volunteering, or previous roles). * Familiarity with ecological survey methods and reporting. * Interest in developing specialist skills (e.g. ornithology, freshwater ecology, protected species). * Membership (or working towards membership) of a relevant professional body (e.g. CIEEM). * Basic GIS or data handling skills. **Behaviours \& Attributes** * Curious and keen to learn. * Reliable, organised, and proactive. * Positive attitude to feedback and development. * Commitment to high standards of safety and quality. * Passion for ecology and environmental protection. **Reporting \& Support** * Reports to: Senior Ecologist / Principal Ecologist / Ecology Team Lead * Works closely with: CPD project teams, EIA practitioners, and other technical disciplines **Why Join ERM as a Junior Ecologist** This role offers structured development within a leading global environmental consultancy, exposure to nationally significant projects, and a clear pathway for progression to Consultant and Senior Ecologist roles. Candidates may be invited to complete an online assessment as part of our recruitment process. All personal information will be handled confidentially and in compliance with data protection laws. ⠀ ERM is committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to thrive, it’s an essential part of what makes ERM a great place to build a career and helps us create better solutions for our clients. We welcome talent from all backgrounds and provide equal opportunities for every candidate. If you have a disability, are neurodivergent, or need accommodations during the selection process, we’re here to support you. Our commitment doesn’t stop at hiring. Once you join us, we’ll ensure you have the tools, support, and adjustments needed to succeed and feel a true sense of belonging. Learn more about our Diversity, Inclusion \& Belonging (DIB) efforts by visiting our website or exploring our 2025 Sustainability Report..

title: Junior Finance & Operations Administrator (Spanish & English)
description: SP Spareparts is an international B2B company specialized in supplying industrial spare parts to customers across Europe and the United States. We are looking for a **Junior Finance \& Operations Administrator** to support our daily administrative and operational activities from our Dublin office. The role involves supporting end\-to\-end order management, from quotation to delivery, including preparing and sending quotations, processing customer orders, and placing purchase orders with suppliers. You will coordinate shipments, monitor delivery timelines, and ensure smooth communication between customers, suppliers, and internal teams. You will also assist with basic finance and administrative tasks such as invoice checks, order updates in internal systems, data entry, and resolving operational or delivery\-related issues. Accuracy, organisation, and attention to detail are key, as you will be handling multiple processes simultaneously in a fast\-paced international environment. We are looking for a **junior profile** with some experience in administration, back office, customer service, logistics coordination, or order management. This is an entry\-level role, so full training will be provided. The ideal candidate is eager to learn, highly organised, comfortable working with digital tools and systems, and able to communicate clearly in both English and Spanish. Fluent English and Spanish are required. The successful candidate should already be based in Dublin and interested in building a long\-term career in operations, administration, and finance within an international company. This is a full\-time position based in Dublin, offering hands\-on responsibility, structured training, and the opportunity to grow within the company.

title: Junior Full Stack Developer
description: Job Purpose: **About RWS** RWS is a global AI solutions company empowering the world’s most trusted enterprise AI. Our proprietary Cultural Intelligence Layer, powered by 250,000 data specialists, cultural and language experts and deep domain professionals, backed by 45\+ patents, makes enterprise AI culturally fluent, contextually accurate and secure, ensuring every interaction reflects a brand’s tone, context and customer values. Through our Generate, Transform and Protect segments, we deliver intelligent content, enterprise knowledge, large\-scale localization and IP protection for global growth. Trusted by 80\+ of the world’s top 100 brands, RWS provides the confidence, governance and expertise organizations need to deploy AI safely, responsibly and at scale. Headquartered in the UK, RWS is listed on AIM (RWS.L). More information: rws.com. **About Propylon** Propylon is the Dublin\-based content‑management arm within the RWS group, specialising in software for legal and regulatory workflows. For more than 20 years, Propylon has helped governments, standard bodies and regulated organisations manage legislation, regulations and legal content in a smarter, modular way. Joining Propylon means working in a people\-led, innovation\-driven environment recognised as one of Ireland’s Best Workplaces in Tech \- while having the backbone and global reach of RWS. You’d be contributing to meaningful projects that shape law, compliance and global content workflows, with the support and flexibility to grow your skills and career. **Job Purpose** We have an exciting opportunity for a Junior Full Stack Developer to join our team in expanding our core product offering and working across major client projects. The candidate will be an experienced engineer with several years of experience in a similar role and will have worked with modern frontend and backend applications in a professional context. Job Overview: **Responsibilities:** * Collaborate with senior engineers and technical leads to help design and build application features * Contribute to backend and frontend development tasks across the platform * Support implementation of product architecture and technical improvements * Participate in code reviews and team discussions to learn engineering best practices * Write clean, maintainable, and well\-tested code * Troubleshoot bugs and help improve application performance and reliability * Work closely with cross\-functional teams to deliver high\-quality product features **Requirements:** * Good understanding of the Python programming language and core programming concepts * Professional or project experience with a Python web framework, preferably Django * Familiarity with SQL\-based relational databases such as PostgreSQL or MySQL * Understanding of REST APIs and how web applications communicate with backend services * Exposure to modern Python features such as type hinting, async programming, or data classes * Familiarity with Git and collaborative development workflows * Basic understanding of Docker or containerization concepts * Strong willingness to learn and grow within a collaborative engineering team **Nice to haves:** * Exposure to a modern JavaScript framework such as React * Familiarity with XML processing or transformation * Interest in document management or workflow systems * Awareness of CI/CD pipelines and DevOps practices **Personal Attributes:** * Enthusiastic about building high\-quality software products * Eager to learn from teammates and contribute to a collaborative team culture * Curious about technology and motivated to continuously improve technical skills * Strong communication and problem\-solving skills * Positive attitude and willingness to take ownership of tasks and deliverables Life at RWS: **Life at RWS** **\-** If you like the idea of working with smart people who are passionate about growing the value of ideas, data and content by making sure organizations are understood, then you’ll love life at RWS. Our purpose is to unlock global understanding. This means our work fundamentally recognizes the value of every language and culture. So, we celebrate difference, we are inclusive and believe that diversity makes us strong. We want every employee to grow as an individual and excel in their career. In return, we expect all our people to live by the values that unite us: **to partner**, putting clients fist and winning together**, to pioneer**, innovating fearlessly and leading with vision and courage, **to progress**, aiming high and growing through actions and **to deliver**, owning the outcome and building trust with our colleagues and clients. **RWS embraces DEI** and promotes equal opportunity, we are an Equal Opportunity Employer and prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind. RWS is committed to the principle of equal employment opportunity for all employees and to providing employees with a work environment free of discrimination and harassment. All employment decisions at RWS are based on business needs, job requirements and individual qualifications, without regard to race, religion, nationality, ethnicity, sex, age, disability, or sexual orientation. RWS will not tolerate discrimination based on any of these characteristics. **RWS Values** Get the 3Ps right – Partner, Pioneer, Progress – and we´ll Deliver together as RWS. **Recruitment Agencies:** RWS Holdings PLC does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward any unsolicited resumes to any RWS employees. Any unsolicited resume received will be treated as the property of RWS and Terms \& Conditions associated with the use of such resume will be considered null and void. \#LI\-MS1 \#LI\-Hybrid

Familiarity with SQL\-based relational databases such as PostgreSQL or MySQL * Understanding of REST APIs and how web applications communicate with backend services * Exposure to modern Python features such as type hinting, async programming, or data classes * Familiarity with Git and collaborative development workflows * Basic understanding of Docker or containerization concepts * Strong willingness to learn and grow within a collaborative engineering team **Nice to haves:** * Exposure to a modern JavaScript framework such as React * Familiarity with XML processing or transformation * Interest in document management or workflow systems * Awareness of CI/CD pipelines and DevOps practices **Personal Attributes:** * Enthusiastic about building high\-quality software products * Eager to learn from teammates and contribute to a collaborative team culture * Curious about technology and motivated to continuously improve technical skills * Strong communication and problem\-solving skills * Positive attitude and willingness to take ownership of tasks and deliverables **Life at RWS** **Life at RWS** **\-** If you like the idea of working with smart people who are passionate about growing the value of ideas, data and content by making sure organizations are understood , then you’ll love life at RWS. Our purpose is to unlock global understanding. This means our work fundamentally recognizes the value of every language and culture. So, we celebrate difference, we are inclusive and believe that diversity makes us strong. We want every employee to grow as an individual and excel in their career. In return, we expect all our people to live by the values that unite us: **to partner** , putting clients fist and winning together **, to pioneer** , innovating fearlessly and leading with vision and courage, **to progress** , aiming high and growing through actions and **to deliver** , owning the outcome and building trust with our colleagues and clients. **RWS embraces DEI** and promotes equal opportunity, we are an Equal Opportunity Employer and prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind. Any unsolicited resume received will be treated as the property of RWS and Terms \& Conditions associated with the use of such resume will be considered null and void.

title: Junior Full Stack Engineer
description: Job Description: EXL (NASDAQ: EXLS) is a global data and artificial intelligence ("AI") company that offers services and solutions to reinvent client business models, drive better outcomes and unlock growth with speed. EXL harnesses the power of data, AI, and deep industry knowledge to transform businesses, including the world’s leading corporations in industries including insurance, healthcare, banking and financial services, media and retail, among others. EXL was founded in 1999 with the core values of innovation, collaboration, excellence, integrity and respect. We are headquartered in New York and have more than 60,000 employees spanning six continents. For more information, visit www.exlservice.com . **Role Title:** Full Stack Engineer (Junior level) **BU/Segment:** Digital **Location:** Dublin, Republic of Ireland (Flexible hybrid working) **Employment Type:** Permanent **As part of your duties, you will be responsible for:** * Design, develop, and maintain full\-stack applications using Python (backend) and React.js (frontend) * Develop APIs and microservices to support AI agent communications and data exchange * Implement real\-time features for agent monitoring, logging, and performance tracking * Troubleshoot and resolve technical issues across the full stack * Work closely with product managers, designers, and other engineers to deliver high\-quality solutions * Write clean, maintainable, and efficient code that prioritizes security and data protection. * Maintain high software quality standards within the team by adhering to best practices. Identify and encourage areas for growth and improvement within the group. * Work in an agile delivery/ DevOps methodology to deliver enterprise\-grade production implementation in iterative sprints. * Explore and leverage AI tools to help improve innovation, efficiency and quality across the SDLC. **Qualifications and experience we consider to be essential for the role:** * Minimum 1\+ Years of experience in application development \& support. * Experience in any programming language. * Experience with any frontend technology like react.js etc. * Understanding of any of the cloud platforms (AWS/Azure /GCP), * Knowledge of algorithms, design patterns, and fundamental computer science concepts \& data structures * Experience with CI/CD pipelines **Good to have (optional)** * Experience with AI/ML libraries such as LangChain, OpenAI API, Bedrock, or similar framework * Understanding of containerization (Docker, Kubernetes), and infrastructure\-as\-code (Terraform, CDK). **Skills and Personal attributes we would like to have:** * Strong communication skills and ability to work with various stakeholders across various geographies * Master's or bachelor's degree, preferably from an Engineering Background * Be a self\-motivated, curious, and productive under high pressure **As part of a leading global Data and AI company, you can look forward to:** * A competitive salary with a generous bonus, private healthcare, life assurance at 4 x your annual salary, income protection insurance, and a rewarding pension. * At **EXL** , we are committed to providing our employees with the tools and resources they need to succeed and excel in their careers. We offer a wide range of professional and personal development opportunities. We also support a range of learning initiatives that allow our employees to build on their existing skills and knowledge. From online courses to seminars and workshops, our employees have the opportunity to enhance their skills and stay up to date with the latest trends and technologies. * As an Equal Opportunity Employer, **EXL** is committed to diversity. Our company does not discriminate based on race, religion, colour, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, age, or disability status. * At **EXL** , we offer a flexible hybrid working model that allows employees to live a balanced, healthy lifestyle while strengthening our culture of collaboration. **To be considered for this role, you must already be eligible to work in the Republic of Ireland.**

title: Junior Fund Accountant
description: **About Us – Opening Up Wealth Together.** FNZ Group believes everyone can create a better life by saving, investing, and managing their money well. We know the foundation to do that already exists in the wealth management industry, but complexity holds firms back. We created wealth’s growth platform to help. We partner with over 650 financial institutions and 12,000 wealth managers, with US$1\.5 trillion in assets under administration (AUA). Together with our customers, we help over 24 million people from all wealth segments to invest in their future. **The Role** Junior Fund Accountant (14\-Month Fixed Term) Joining a team ranging in experience from recent graduates, through to colleagues who have progressed internally to professional qualification, this is an excellent career development opportunity. **The Key Responsibilities Of The Department Include** * Providing a high standard Fund Accounting service to FNZ Ireland’s clients * Valuing the funds in an accurate and timely manner and publication of Net Asset Values (NAVs) * Placing trades with international fund managers in accordance with the advice of the clients’ asset allocation advisers * Purchasing and selling foreign currencies in a manner approved by the client. * Treasury management of cash holdings * Recording all fund transactions in an accurate and timely manner * Preparation of all operational team output for capture on our imaging system * Producing monthly accounts and other data for financial and statutory returns * Performing reconciliations to confirm accuracy of accounts information. * Liaising with clients, fund managers and banks daily * Utilising the firm's technology solutions to manage client enquiries efficiently and effectively. **The Person** The ideal candidate will have an excellent academic record, the ability to work in a fast\-moving environment and the interpersonal skills required to interact with colleagues, clients, and international financial institutions. They should also have: * Strong aptitude for numerical accuracy and a high degree of analytical and problem\-solving ability. * 2\.1\- or first\-class honours degree ideally in a relevant discipline such a Commerce, Business Studies, Economics or Maths / Maths Related (however graduates from other disciplines wishing for a career change will also be considered if they have a strong maths aptitude) * A working knowledge of MS Excel, SQL and Data Analytics would be advantageous. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills * Ability to work well within a team environment. * An ambition to develop a successful and rewarding career. * An ability to work under pressure and meet challenging deadlines. * An organised and systematic approach to their job * A strong customer service orientation * Knowledge of a European language would be advantageous but not essential. * Knowledge of Fund Accounting would be advantageous but not essential. **About FNZ** *FNZ is committed to opening up wealth so that everyone, everywhere can invest in their future on their terms. We know the foundation to do that already exists in the wealth management industry, but complexity holds firms back.* *We created wealth’s growth platform to help. We provide a global, end\-to\-end wealth management platform that integrates modern technology with business and investment operations. All in a regulated financial institution.* *We partner with the world’s leading financial institutions, with over US$2\.4 trillion in assets on platform (AoP). Together with our clients, we empower nearly 30 million people across all wealth segments to invest in their future.*

title: Junior HR Administrator
description: The Royal Marine Hotel is recruiting a Junior Human Resources Administrator. This role supports the day to day work of the HR department and helps keep things running smoothly for employees across the hotel. **This position offers up to 32 hours per week, depending on business needs.** **Role Overview** The position involves general HR administration, including maintaining employee records, assisting with hiring and supporting staff throughout their employment. The role also includes basic health and safety administration and ensuring procedures are followed correctly. **Key Responsibilities** * Keep employee files up to date, both paper and digital * Assist with job ads, reviewing applications, arranging and conducting interviews * Prepare paperwork for new starters and organise inductions * Handle leaver paperwork and update records when staff leave * Work with payroll by checking weekly hours and updating employee details * Respond to day to day staff queries and pass on issues where needed * Help organise and conduct training sessions and maintain training records * Support health and safety administration tasks * Support and conduct internal staff events, recognition activities and social initiatives across the hotel **Requirements** * Experience in an administrative role, ideally within HR or a busy office environment * Experience handling employee records, documentation, or similar administrative processes * Familiar with basic HR practices such as recruitment admin, onboarding paperwork and employee files * Comfortable using Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook) and database/HR systems * Ability to manage multiple tasks at once and keep work organised without constant supervision * Strong written and verbal communication skills, including dealing with staff queries in a clear and professional way * High level of accuracy when working with records and confidential information * Previous experience in a hotel or hospitality setting would be an advantage **Benefits** * Opportunities for progression * Staff discounts across sister properties * Health club and pool membership * Discounted spa treatments and products * Complimentary golf in Druids Heath * Bike to work scheme * Free parking * Meals on shift and healthy snacks * Employee recognition programmes * Employee referral bonus

title: Junior Marketing Assistant
description: Part\-Time Marketing Assistant **KBB Digital – Dublin / Hybrid** About KBB Digital KBB Digital is a specialist digital marketing agency serving the kitchen, bedroom, bathroom, joinery, and construction industries. We work with premium brands and independent businesses to help grow their online presence through social media marketing, content creation, photography, videography, website development, and AI\-powered marketing solutions. Due to continued growth, we are looking for a creative and motivated **Part\-Time Marketing Assistant** to join our team. Role Overview This is an exciting opportunity for someone with a passion for digital marketing, social media, and content creation. The successful candidate will support the management of existing customer accounts while also helping grow KBB Digital’s client base. The role involves creating engaging social media content, assisting with photography and videography projects, supporting website updates, and leveraging platforms such as Meta, LinkedIn, and AI\-based marketing tools to drive results for clients. Key ResponsibilitiesSocial Media Management * Manage and schedule content across Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn * Create and publish a mix of posts, reels, stories, and promotional content * Maintain consistent branding and engagement across multiple client accounts * Monitor social media performance and identify opportunities for growth * Assist in developing creative content strategies for clients Content Creation * Support photography and videography shoots for client projects * Edit and repurpose content for social media and web use * Organise and manage existing client content libraries * Create marketing assets using Canva, Adobe tools, and AI\-assisted platforms Website \& Digital Support * Assist with ongoing website updates and portfolio uploads * Support content population for new website projects * Work alongside the team on AI\-supported website development initiatives Business Development Support * Assist in identifying and onboarding new customers * Support marketing campaigns aimed at growing KBB Digital’s customer base * Help prepare marketing materials and proposals **Ideal Candidate** We are looking for someone who is: * Creative, organised, and self\-motivated * Passionate about social media and digital marketing * Comfortable working across multiple projects and brands * Interested in interiors, design, construction, or creative industries * Keen to learn and use emerging AI marketing tools Skills \& ExperienceEssential * Experience managing social media platforms (Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn) * Strong content creation skills * Good understanding of Meta Business Suite and LinkedIn * Basic photography/video editing experience * Excellent communication and organisational skills Desirable * Experience with Canva, Adobe Creative Suite, or CapCut * Knowledge of WordPress or website content management * Understanding of AI marketing tools and automation platforms * Experience running paid social campaigns Role Details * Part\-time position * Flexible/hybrid working options available * Opportunity to grow with an expanding agency * Work with leading brands in the KBB and construction sectors How to Apply Please send your CV, portfolio/examples of social media work, and a short introduction outlining your experience and interest in the role. Pay: €15\.00\-€20\.00 per hour Work Location: Hybrid remote in Dublin, County Dublin

title: Junior Supply Chain Consultant - September 2026
description: **Job Description** **About the role** **Kickstart your consulting career!** Get ready to launch your career in consulting, and join us! You’ll have a spot in our supply chain training boot camp at the beginning of September. You’ll sharpen your consulting expertise alongside fellow new starters from around the globe. Our training program covers everything from essential soft skills to advanced supply chain management to becoming a kick\-ass Supply Chain Consultant. Afterwards, you're ready to start on one of our running projects. **What can you do at Bluecrux?** * You'll start immediately in a visible, autonomous role as a project member in one of our running value chain projects in industries such as life sciences, chemicals, food and beverages, and manufacturing. * You'll see yourself working on and contributing to the main project phases. Here's an idea of what to expect: + Analyze and Optimize: You will always analyze, map, and conduct existing Supply Chain and Operations business processes. + Collaborate: Work closely with clients to understand their needs and make a real impact. Think about preparing and conducting project interviews to understand the client's needs better. You will become aware of the customer organization and be part of the change in their organization and ways of working. + Track and report: You'll track KPIs and report them accurately to the customer project team, ensuring clear, actionable insights. + Document and deliver: You create the most important customer and project deliverables, such as top\-notch project notes, guides and training material. * Here’s how you’ll continuously be making an impact: + Tell us, what’s your flavour? All of our projects share a common transformation mission, and once you join a project, you’ll be introduced to different extra layers of specialisation to add value. We will encourage you to explore and accelerate in Technology, Process knowledge, Data or Change Management. **Job Requirements** **About you** * You will graduate before September 2026 and want to start your career at the beginning of September 2026\. * You see yourself thriving in the world of value chain transformation and are interested in how Technology, Business Processes, Data Analytics, and Change management create a flourishing ecosystem of solutions. * You feel connected with and are ready to represent our company values: dig deep, own it, come together, move fast and be kind! * Above all, we believe in your potential: + You can demonstrate profound logical reasoning, and you are resourceful in detailing solutions and considerate about their consequences. + You're a team worker and player who can perform well without much supervision and who is committed to your own objectives. + You're open to feedback and always look for ways to enhance collaboration. + You like to be challenged daily, show proactive ownership and, above all, thrive in a fast\-paced environment. * Some hard skills: + Ideally, you have had an introduction to Supply Chain Processes in a minor or major within a master’s degree program, such as business engineering, civil engineering—operational research, applied economics, mechanical engineering or an MBA program. + You are fluent in English, the official work language in all of our offices. Mastery of any other language is a definite plus. + Willingness and eligibility to travel globally and to work in a hybrid working model. **Why join us?** * A kickstart to your career through our Supply Chain Bootcamp training program, at the beginning of September. You will learn the ins \& outs of Supply Chain Management: Demand Planning, S\&OP and Financial Planning, Supply Planning, Inventory Management, Master Data Management, Supply Chain Modelling and Planning Tools. This will allow you to onboard into our projects with a backpack full of expertise \& skills! * We know you by your first name, and don't let titles or levels withhold us from being truly aware of your talents. * You'll work on international projects and travel to client sites. From the start, you will gain exposure and recognition for every achievement at Bluecrux. * A Great Place To Work with lots of growth opportunities. We encourage initiative, so you’ll learn a lot quickly. Sometimes, you’ll fail fast, but we’ll help you to get up. We’ll enhance your personal and professional skills, and you can count on a great, supportive team of hands\-on, inspiring and enthusiastic supply chain colleagues. * Team events and fun include yearly Bluecrux weekends, last Friday meetings with drinks, team building, and sports activities. * Enjoy a flexible \& hybrid work environment, combining remote work, office presence and onsite client visits. * A competitive salary package, bonus program, net allowances, a smartphone with phone subscription and other benefits (such as a fridge full of drinks \& healthy snacks). **About us** At Bluecrux, we transform supply chains into smart, efficient value chains through a blend of expert consulting and cutting\-edge technology. We collaborate closely with industry leaders like Johnson \& Johnson, GSK, Bridgestone, AkzoNobel, Beiersdorf, Oatly, and many more – creating partnerships that drive meaningful impact. With a global presence in Europe, the US and APAC, we're committed to shaping the future of value chains, one success story at a time.

title: Legal Counsel – Multinational Tech Company
description: My client is looking for an experienced Legal Counsel to join their team in Dublin. They are a major Multinational company growing their team in Ireland at scale. You’ll provide legal advice to management on strategic initiatives, business operations, regulatory matters, and compliance in Ireland. Lead corporate legal activities and ensure timely, effective legal support across the business, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts. **Key Responsibilities** * Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial contracts and legal documents. * Advise on contractual, statutory, and regulatory obligations, ensuring ongoing compliance in customer engagements. * Conduct legal due diligence and research to support business decisions. * Provide legal counsel across corporate, commercial, and risk management matters. * Identify and mitigate legal and operational risks. * Interpret statutes, regulations, and contracts, and advise on resolving legal and business issues. * Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver clear, practical legal guidance. * Manage and support legal projects and, where required, supervise junior legal staff. * Report regularly to the Head of Legal on key matters and developments. **Skills \& Experience** * Qualified lawyer (LLB/LLM/JD or equivalent) with a current practising certificate. * 5–10 years’ experience, including at least 5 years in outsourcing, IT contracts, or licensing. * Strong drafting, negotiation, and analytical skills, with solid knowledge of relevant laws. * Effective communicator with the ability to work across all levels and cultures. * Strong organisational, project management, and prioritisation skills. * Collaborative, proactive, and able to operate independently with minimal support. * Experience managing or mentoring team members is an advantage. * Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required. Salary dependent on candidate experience. Benefits: Annual Bonus Scheme. Contributory Pension. Private Medical Insurance. Life Assurance \& Long\-Term Disability. Employee Assistance Programme. 22 days annual leave \+ 10 public holidays. Continuous Learning \& Development. Access to extensive training \& certification resources. Lunch \& Learn sessions. Additional perks including company discounts, on\-site parking, and bike\-to\-work scheme **Based in Dublin, Ireland. Hybrid.** Candidates must be eligible to work in Ireland/EU. For more information, please contact **David Coyle** at **01 635 1748** or email **david@methodius.com**

title: Loss Prevention Internal Control Officer
description: **Role** The internal controls officer supports the loss prevention manager. This role involves operational \& administration tasks focused on stock management across the owned store estate, database management, and cross collaboration with all departments to ensure effective loss prevention strategies \& stock loss compliance. The role requires the candidate have extensive knowledge on retail stock and ordering best practice and can actively work closely with our colleagues and internal teams to ensure stock holding targets are being maintained whilst reducing stock loss. **Who should apply?** This position is ideal for someone who: * **Has a passion and deep knowledge of best practice stock management –** you naturally enjoy implementing best practice systems at store level to ensure all aspects of stock are managed to mitigate stock loss * **Has an investigative mindset** – you naturally enjoy analysing information, spotting inconsistencies, and uncovering root causes. Experience with investigative techniques is a strong advantage. * **Is highly discreet and trustworthy** – confidentiality is essential. You must be dependable, discreet, and capable of handling sensitive information with absolute professionalism. * **Is detail‑driven and thorough** – you notice the small things that others may miss and are committed to accuracy in reporting and documentation. * **Communicates clearly and confidently** – you can engage with colleagues at all levels, from store teams to senior management, and present findings in a clear, structured manner. * **Thrives in a fast‑paced environment** – you can manage multiple tasks, prioritise effectively, and adapt to changing situations. * **Is proactive and solution‑focused** – you look ahead, identify risks early, and help develop practical solutions to prevent issues before they occur. * **Values teamwork and collaboration** – you work well with others, support colleagues, and contribute positively to a culture of safety and compliance. **Key performance indicators (but not limited to)** **Compliance** * Lead out on \& collaborate on set stock holding targets by site working with our commercial team identifying risk lines / dead stock lines and bespoke ranges by store. * Lead out on managing expired levels in stores by identifying trends \& root causes and working closely with the commercial team to eliminate re\-occurrence. * Lead out on actively closing back areas in selected stores that are not needed for stock storage ensuring order to need is being implemented. * Supporting the ICO in compliance on fire drills and fire wardens training. * Supporting the ICO colleague training such as manual handling and cash handling policy. * Supporting the ICO on owned store data base on fire and security legal requirement compliance and escalating non\-compliance were applicable. * Maintain \& have available accurate records of stores incidents \& investigations in conjunction with lpm as to mitigate company liability \& and comply with human resource policy. **People** * Lead out on \-site training in stock \& ordering both for front of store and RX – track and report on same. * Lead out on owned stores in hitting their stock holding targets by actively monitoring, reporting, and working closely with the commercial team and store colleagues * Support the ICO in owned stores in implementing and training cash handling policy \& loss preventions strategies were needed. * Support the ICO for till users and access levels ensuring consistency maintained. * Support the ICO owned store data base on key holding ensuring constantly maintained and accurate records. * Support \& assist \& maintain records of security related training such as but not limited too – store open / close, theft \& incidents \& cash handling. **Operational** * Support LPM when required in carrying out store audits for compliance such as cash / theft and security \& stock holding. * Support when required in the implementation of loss prevention measures \& processes in stores. * Support the up skilling of store colleagues on any new processes and procedures being implemented. * Support the ICO in the reporting \& monitoring on cash working closely with our cash \& banking division to ensure stores are not exceeding cash holdings target and report noncompliance monthly. **Loss prevention** * Lead out on identifying \& reporting on stock inventory metrics in line with policy \& processes \& stock holding targets. * Lead out on reporting and monitoring expired / damaged / theft and ensure processes in place for accurate recording and identifying trends for potential fraud in relation to stock recording. * Support the ICO in identifying \& reporting loss trends in relation to cash / safe balances were needed. * Support the lpm were required potential fraud related investigations * Assist \& support lpm in producing / updating \& maintaining company sop’s \& official policy \& procedures in relation to LP, maintain database for same were required. * Support the lpm in undertaking a clean\-up of all existing and or historical sops / policies that are redundant and maintain 1 database centrally were needed. * Attend required meetings \& build consensus / cross collaboration working with all stakeholders to ensure goals and objectives are being met. **Uniphar – who we are** With a workforce of more than 3000 employees spread across Ireland, United Kingdom, mainland Europe, mean, and the USA, Uniphar is a trusted global partner to pharma and MedTech manufacturers, working to improve patient access to medicines around the world. Uniphar provides outsourced and specialised services to its clients, leveraging the strong relationships with 200\+ of the world’s best known pharmaco\-medical manufacturers across multiple geographies, enabled by our cutting\-edge digital technology and our highly expert teams. Uniphar is organised into three key divisions: supply chain retail \& on demand, Uniphar med tech and Uniphar pharma. Click here to find out more: about us \- who we are \| Uniphar **Culture at Uniphar** Uniphar is a combination of many businesses each with its own identity, culture, values, and ways of working. Our overarching culture narrative is inclusive of each of these stores. Uniphar lives and breathes four key pillars which unpin and support our culture. We have a people first approach \- we do the right thing. We support entrepreneurial spirit where adaptability, commitment \& resilience is embodied in our way of working. We have a common purpose that connects our diversified businesses and people. Finally, trust is at the heart of how we operate. Everything Uniphar does is enabled by our people and as we continue to grow domestically and internationally, we become more diverse. This diversity fuels our business and culture. **Sustainability** Sustainability is at the core of what we do and is deeply embedded in our business strategy. We want to contribute positively to the people and the world around us. Running our business in a sustainable way ensures that Uniphar will continue to prosper in the long\-term. We are focused on ensuring that each of the five pillars of our sustainability strategy are a fundamental part of our decision\-making process. *Please note that Uniphar is an equal\-opportunity employer; we do not discriminate and welcome all responses.* \#unipharsupplychainandretail \#unipharjobs
